item.title: Australia Watchdog Says Money Launderers Ramping Up AI for Scams
item.description: Australia’s financial crimes watchdog warned of a heightened threat of money laundering linked to artificial intelligence that has been used by crooks to scale up activities, automate processes and create fake documents.

item.title: Trump Delays Move to Lower Tariffs on Beef Imports, WSJ Says
item.description: The Trump administration is delaying a plan to suspend tariffs on imported beef, according to the Wall Street Journal.

item.title: Bond Selloff Points to Rate-Hike Jolt for Indian Equities Ahead
item.description: Modi’s call to curb fuel use suggests an imminent hike in pump prices, adding to inflationary pressure.

item.title: JX Metals Shares Drop Most Since April 2025 on Convertible Bond
item.description: Shares of Japanese metals refiner JX Advanced Metals Corp. slid after the company unveiled a plan to issue ¥250 billion ($1.6 billion) of bonds that can be converted into stocks to fund share buybacks.

item.title: Korea Roils Markets by Floating ‘Citizen Dividend’ From AI Tax
item.description: A top South Korean policymaker said the nation should pay citizens a “dividend” using taxes on AI profits, underscoring growing pressure on authorities to redistribute gains from a boom that has enriched chipmakers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.

item.title: Franklin Templeton Turns to Fixed Income to Fuel India Growth
item.description: Franklin Templeton is betting that Indian investors’ rising interest in fixed income will power the next leg of its growth in the country, where it is regaining ground after a credit crunch six years ago.

item.title: DroneShield Plunges as Regulator Probes Filings, Share Trades
item.description: DroneShield Ltd.’s stock tumbled to the lowest in about three months after the Australian counter-drone firm said Australia’s financial regulator was reviewing disclosures and share trades from November.

item.title: BOJ Signals Chance of Rate Hike Next Month Over Inflation Risks
item.description: The Bank of Japan signaled the possibility of a hike to its benchmark rate next month with a summary of views from last month’s board meeting that conveyed concerns over upside inflation risks stemming from the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Central Banks Tap Most Yuan Swap Lines With PBOC in Two Years
item.description: Global central banks’ use of the People’s Bank of China’s swap lines reached a two‑year high in the first quarter, underscoring rising international demand for the Chinese currency.

item.title: Kalshi Judge Predicts Tribe Will Win Block on Sports Contracts
item.description: A tribal nation is likely to succeed in blocking Kalshi from offering sports contracts on its land, a federal judge said in what appears to be the first ruling of its kind against the prediction market operator.

item.title: Japan’s Reserves Tally Likely Too Early to Reflect Intervention
item.description: Japan’s foreign currency reserves at the end of April were largely unchanged from the previous month, likely an indication that currency intervention came too close to the end of the month to be reflected in the data.

item.title: Petrobras Misses Profit Estimates Despite War-Driven Oil Rally
item.description: Brazil’s state-controlled oil company Petrobras fell short of earnings estimates as it held domestic gasoline prices stable during the war-driven price surge.

item.title: Japan’s Households Cut Spending Even as Wages Kept Growing
item.description: Japan’s household spending fell for a fourth consecutive month even as wages continued to grow, highlighting the fragility of consumption.

item.title: Gold Steady as Traders Track Hormuz Stalemate, Inflation Risks
item.description: Gold was steady as traders assessed the deadlock in the Strait of Hormuz that has sent oil prices higher and added further fuel to inflation concerns.

item.title: GameStop Shares Roiled by Short-Lived Roaring Kitty Posts
item.description: GameStop Corp. shares spiked and then quickly dropped in postmarket trading Monday after cryptic social media posts showed up and then disappeared from the social media account of Keith Gill, the financial influencer known as “Roaring Kitty,” who became prominent during the 2021 meme-stock craze.

item.title: US Unleashes Another Wave of Emergency Oil as Gas Prices Bite
item.description: The US Strategic Petroleum Reserve awarded 53.3 million barrels to companies including oil trader Trafigura Group and US refiner Marathon Petroleum Corp, adding to a wave of oil released to help tame surging prices stemming from the Iran war.

item.title: Gen Z Shoppers Driving Traffic Growth at Simon’s Malls, CEO Says
item.description: Mall owner Simon Property Group Inc. is seeing strong interest from Gen Z shoppers and the brands that target them, Chief Executive Officer Eli Simon said on a call with investors.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain as Trump Casts Doubt Over Ceasefire With Iran
item.description: Oil steadied as US President Donald Trump cast doubt over the ceasefire with Iran after rejecting Tehran’s latest peace offer, prolonging the effective closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise, Oil Gains on Iran Deadlock: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were set to rise Tuesday, tracking Wall Street gains as investors remained bullish even as the US and Iran failed to reach terms to end their conflict.

item.title: China Investors Bet Xi-Trump Meet to Extend Trade-Detente Rally
item.description: China investors are counting on the summit between Xi Jinping and Donald Trump to deliver just enough to sustain the detente trade underpinning stocks and the yuan.

item.title: Sunshine Silver Files for IPO Seeking Funds to Reopen Idaho Mine
item.description: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining Co. filed for a US initial public offering, and is seeking funds to restart a mine in Idaho that previously produced silver, antimony and other minerals.

item.title: Ackman’s Pershing Seeks to Toss Suit Over Howard Hughes Deal
item.description: Pershing Square sought to toss what it called a “facially defective” shareholder lawsuit accusing founder Bill Ackman of bullying Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. directors into a deal that increased the investment firm’s stake at an unfairly low price.

item.title: GitLab Says Will Cut Jobs to Spend on Growth in ‘Agentic Era’
item.description: GitLab Inc., which makes software that helps developers on coding projects, said it is cutting jobs to free up money to spend on the market opportunity for artificial intelligence agents.

item.title: Australia to Tackle Housing Tax as Budget Deficit Set to Narrow
item.description: Australia’s center-left government is expected to crack down on tax concession for property investors as part of efforts to ease generational inequality and help rein in a budget deficit.

item.title: Larry Fink ‘Quite Bullish’ on Opportunity to Invest in Venezuela
item.description: BlackRock Inc. Chief Executive Officer Larry Fink expressed optimism about investing in Venezuela following the overhaul sparked by Nicolas Maduro’s removal.

item.title: Software Firm ServiceNow Plans to Raise $4 Billion in Bond Sale
item.description: Software company ServiceNow Inc. is looking to raise about $4 billion from a potential US high-grade bond sale tied to the software firm’s recent acquisitions.

item.title: Hims Sales Miss on Rising Competition for Weight-Loss Drugs
item.description: Hims & Hers Health Inc. reported a first-quarter loss and sales that missed Wall Street estimates amid increasing competition in the weight-loss drug market.

item.title: Advent, ADIA-Backed Power Equipment Firm Innio Files for IPO
item.description: Gas engine manufacturer Innio Holding GmbH filed for a US initial public offering, joining a slew of industrial companies looking to tap the surge in data center spending.

item.title: Microsoft Targeted $92 Billion Return on Early OpenAI Investment
item.description: Microsoft Corp. targeted a $92 billion return from its large early investments in OpenAI, a landmark arrangement that helped usher in the current AI era.

item.title: Radio Station Owner Spanish Broadcasting Files for Bankruptcy
item.description: Radio station owner Spanish Broadcasting System Inc. filed for bankruptcy on Monday after announcing plans to hand ownership to noteholders in exchange for restructuring the debt.
